meta.name: Europa Universalis - 1444
meta.author: Historia
meta.description: November 11, 1444. The Fall of Varna has shaken Christendom. The Ottoman Empire rises in the East. The Hundred Years' War enters its final phase. The Age of Exploration beckons.

ruler.name: Ruler of Expansionist Kingdom of Merina

armies.name: Army of Expansionist Kingdom of Merina

ruler.name: Ruler of Andean states and chiefdoms

armies.name: Army of Andean states and chiefdoms

ruler.name: Ruler of minor Hindu and Buddhist kingdoms

armies.name: Army of minor Hindu and Buddhist kingdoms

nodes.name: Fall of Constantinople
nodes.description: The Ottoman Empire conquers Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire.

preventionConditions.description: Byzantium is strong enough to resist

nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the fall of Constantinople to the Ottoman Turks. The last bastion of the Roman Empire has fallen.

nodes.name: End of the Hundred Years' War
nodes.description: France and England sign peace, ending the long conflict. France recovers its territories.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the end of the Hundred Years' War between France and England.

nodes.name: Fall of Granada
nodes.description: Castile completes the Reconquista by conquering the Emirate of Granada, the last Moorish state in Iberia.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the fall of Granada and the completion of the Reconquista after centuries of struggle.

nodes.name: Iberian Wedding
nodes.description: The crowns of Castile and Aragon unite through royal marriage, forming the basis of modern Spain.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the union of Castile and Aragon through the marriage of Ferdinand and Isabella.

nodes.name: Age of Exploration Begins
nodes.description: European powers begin exploring the Atlantic and beyond, seeking new trade routes.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the beginning of the Age of Exploration as Portuguese sailors venture further into the unknown.

nodes.name: Ottoman Balkan Conquest
nodes.description: The Ottoman Empire extends its dominion over the Balkans, subjugating Serbia and Bosnia.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Ottoman expansion into the Balkans following the fall of Constantinople.

nodes.name: Burgundian Succession Crisis
nodes.description: The death of Charles the Bold triggers a succession crisis. France seizes Burgundian territories.

preventionConditions.description: Burgundy forges strong alliances to survive

nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Burgundian succession crisis and France's seizure of Burgundian lands.

nodes.name: Protestant Reformation
nodes.description: Martin Luther's theses spark religious upheaval across Europe, dividing Christendom.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe the Protestant Reformation and its shattering impact on European religious unity.

nodes.name: Italian Wars Begin
nodes.description: France invades Italy, beginning decades of conflict over the Italian peninsula.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe France's invasion of Italy and the beginning of the Italian Wars.

nodes.name: Rise of Muscovy
nodes.description: The Grand Duchy of Moscow expands rapidly, laying the foundations for the Russian Empire.
nodes.narrativePrompt: Describe Muscovy's conquest of Novgorod and its rise as the dominant Russian power.

narrative.introduction: It is November 1444. The Battle of Varna has claimed the life of King Władysław III of Poland and Hungary. Europe stands at a crossroads between the medieval world and the dawn of a new age.
